answer.text: Options of the same type and strike but different expiry dates
Options of the same type and expiry but different strikes
A call and a put at the same strike and expiry
Futures and options on the same underlying

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Horizontal spread = same type, same strike, DIFFERENT expiries. It's a play on the difference in time values decaying at different rates. Payoff chart cannot be drawn as expiries differ. **The Trap:** VERTICAL spread = same expiry, different strikes. DIAGONAL = different expiry AND different strikes. **Cross-Exam Context:** Spread types are tested in Series I and VIII. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Calendar spreads profit from the difference in time decay rates between near and far month options.
